summary: Matched the Star Rod item implementation and its projectile/star article by replacing placeholder stubs with typed C code, declaring item state tables, adding Star Rod Star item-var storage, tightening function prototypes, and marking both objects as Matching in the build configuration. The PR also adjusted GALE01 split boundaries for the newly matched data/sdata/sdata2 sizes.
what_changed: Changed configure.py entries for melee/it/items/itstarrod.c and melee/it/items/itstarrodstar.c from NonMatching to Matching.

Implemented previously placeholder-marked Star Rod functions including spawned/setup helpers, shoot helper it_802923BC, reset/state-change helpers, physics/collision callbacks, pickup/drop/thrown/air callbacks, and collision response callbacks.
Added Star Rod item state table it_803F5F90 with six motion-state entries wired to animation, physics, and collision callbacks.
Added local StarRodAttributes struct in itstarrod.c with an int count-like field and Vec attribute used by it_3F14_Logic22_Spawned and it_80292394.

Implemented Star Rod Star projectile spawning through it_802988E4 using SpawnItem, It_Kind_StarRod_Star, parent fighter gobj fields, Item_80268B18, item-var initialization, it_802989C8 setup, and db_80225DD8 linkage.
Implemented Star Rod Star projectile initialization it_802989C8, selecting speed, hitbox parameter, lifetime/rate setup, and spin direction based on item var starrodstar.x0 and special attributes.
Added Star Rod Star state table it_803F6530 with one motion state.
Implemented Star Rod Star anim/phys/coll callbacks, including decrementing xD44_lifeTimer, rotating the JObj by item var x4, and ending on it_8026DFB0 collision.
Implemented Star Rod Star reflected and shield-bounced callbacks by applying bounce/reflection behavior and then setting JObj Z rotation to atan2f(y velocity, x velocity).
Added itStarRodStar_ItemVars to itCommonItems.h and inserted it as starrodstar in Item.xDD4_itemVar union in src/melee/it/types.h.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations for it_802923F8, it_80292488, it_802988E4, and it_802989C8 with concrete typed prototypes.
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt boundaries for itstarrod.c .sdata2 and itstarrodstar.c .data/.sdata to reflect the matched object layout.
smart_moves: Used the existing item-state-table pattern instead of hardcoding state transitions, which made the Star Rod and Star Rod Star objects fit existing item engine conventions.
Kept article-specific attribute layouts local to the implementation files as StarRodAttributes and StarRodStarAttrs while only promoting persistent runtime vars into common item-var headers.

Modeled the projectile variant flag as itStarRodStar_ItemVars.x0, allowing it_802989C8 to choose between normal and alternate attribute sets for speed, hit size/damage-related parameters, and lifetime setup.
After reflect/shield bounce, recomputed projectile JObj rotation from velocity with atan2f, preserving visual orientation after direction changes.
Zeroed SpawnItem velocity and z position explicitly during Star Rod Star spawn, matching projectile setup semantics visible in the diff.
Updated split metadata alongside Matching toggles, which is important because matched data/sdata sizes changed for these object files.
decomp_lessons: When matching item articles, update both the parent item and spawned projectile/article together if they share calls and runtime item vars; here Star Rod calls it_802988E4 in itstarrodstar.c.
Replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete prototypes as soon as caller/callee relationships reveal argument types; this PR clarified Item_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, Vec*, float, and int parameters.
Persistent item article data that lives in Item.xDD4_itemVar should be added to the item-var union, not left as raw offsets in implementation code.
Item special attributes can often be represented by small local structs when their layout is only needed by one item implementation.
For simple no-op physics callbacks, an explicit `return;` body may be used where it helps match generated code rather than relying on an empty function body.

Collision callbacks frequently return false after invoking engine helpers such as it_8026E15C, it_8026D62C, or it_8026E8C4; preserve return behavior rather than assuming helper return values should propagate.
Matching object files can change data-section byte boundaries, so split files must be checked even when most source changes are code.
naming_conventions: Parent item file uses `itStarrod_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll` for motion callbacks and `it_3F14_Logic22_*` for item logic callbacks.
Projectile file uses `itStarrodstar_UnkMotion0_*` for its single motion state and `it_3F14_Logic36_*` for projectile logic callbacks.
Article attribute structs were named descriptively but locally: `StarRodAttributes` and `StarRodStarAttrs`.
The new union member follows item-var naming style: `itStarRodStar_ItemVars starrodstar`.
Address-named functions remained in hexadecimal style where semantic names were not recovered, such as `it_802923BC`, `it_80292488`, `it_802988E4`, and `it_802989C8`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in Star Rod Star animation/reflection/shield-bounce functions, indicating stack padding was needed to match generated assembly.
Used chained assignment for velocity zeroing (`ip->x40_vel.x = ip->x40_vel.y = ip->x40_vel.z = 0.0f` and similarly for SpawnItem velocity), likely preserving compiler output shape.
Used explicit floating constants such as `0.0f`, `1.0f`, and field-by-field Vec/velocity assignments rather than abstract helpers.
Implemented small callbacks with explicit `return false`, `return true`, or `return;` to match expected control flow.
Used `atan2f` from `<MSL/trigf.h>` and JObj rotation APIs to match projectile orientation behavior after bounce/reflection.
Updated `splits.txt` section ends for .data/.sdata/.sdata2 after matching, showing data layout changed by exact typed declarations.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename `StarRodAttributes`, `StarRodStarAttrs`, or address-named helpers such as `it_802988E4` and `it_802989C8` to semantic names.
Check subsequent item-var union changes around `itStarRodStar_ItemVars` for field naming of `x0` and `x4`.
Search for other projectile item PRs that use `atan2f(ip->x40_vel.y, ip->x40_vel.x)` after reflect or shield bounce.
Compare Star Rod Star spawn setup with other spawned item articles using `SpawnItem`, `Item_80268B18`, and `db_80225DD8`.
Verify whether the local StarRodStarAttrs field meanings were later identified, especially x0/x4/x8/xC/x10/x14/x18/x1C.
